Once the planned work on the 21st of April 2026 has been completed, we will be re-connecting the temporary generator. Once all our planned work has been completed, we will then disconnect the generators on the 23rd of April 2026. This work is taking place near Wrestlingworth Road.

I'm pleased to inform you that we believe our engineers have been able to restore power to all customers by replacing faulted network equipment following a cable being being damaged by a third party working in the Thame Road area. Sent by Aaron at 23:09 - Thanks for your patience this evening.

Our staff are checking our equipment throughout the area to pinpoint the issue on our overhead cabling network. In addition to this, they are liaising closely with our control room to find safe and reliable ways to divert power around any problems and get everyone restored.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them. We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the Smallfield Road area to investigate the power cut. Specialist engineers are also working to divert power remotely, and this can cause your lights to go on and off. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive, even more so if you are vulnerable. If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress but for any concerns or questions, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Hello, Roland from the messaging team here. Thanks for your patience today. 

Our records show that some properties affected by today's power cut may have had other unexpected interruptions recently, and although we don't believe today's issue to be related, we are sorry for further disruption. Power cuts are fully investigated by our engineers, but can sometimes happen multiple times before we can safely find the issue and fix it.

You may not have had other power cuts recently, but if you have, our customer services team will try to update as many customers as possible about our investigations. 

Our staff are still working to get your power back on as quickly as they can, and we'll update you again soon.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Roland at 07:26 - Hi - thanks for bearing with us, I know a power cut can be very disruptive. We've had an update from our staff that there are now 119 properties still affected by the grounded overhead cables, as they have managed to safely divert some power, getting the lights back on for 73 of our customers. We'll keep sending text updates to everyone until all the power to your area is on. To stop receiving texts, just follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the Dereham NR19 1 area and completed their investigation. They have found a grounded overhead cable which will need repairing. To safely repair the fault, we're sending additional engineers to assist. We'll let you know when they arrive on site. You may find your power is going on and off; this is normal for this type of issue and is due to our engineers working to divert power remotely. By doing this, we've restored power to 446 customers. There are now 192 properties still affected and we're estimating all power will be on between 10:00 and 11:00, but this may change depending on what our staff find. To stop receiving texts, follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Sent by Roland at 07:09
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress.

Steps.Message:
Sent by David at 07:19 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the Hackney Grove area, the estimated time of 10:00-11:00 hasn't changed. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive; we understand this can greatly impact your daily activities and we want to support you however we can. We'll update you with all of our progress, but if you need to speak to us, call our team on 08003163105 any time. If you would like more information about what we think the fault is; here's a short YouTube video https://UKPN.short.gy/fuse. Sent by David at 07:08.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by David at 06:36 - Engineers are on their way. At this point we think the issue is a fault with equipment in your local substation in the Hackney Grove area. We didn't know this was going to happen, so time frames we provide throughout are estimated and are subject to change.

Our estimated time to have power back on is currently between 10:00 and 11:00, but after we arrive and assess the situation this may change. The reason for this is there can sometimes be a more complex underlying issue found by our engineer's tests which may take longer, and if not we may get power back on sooner.

All of our texts are written and sent personally by our team, and we'll let you know once our staff arrive.

Steps.Message: We have engineers on their way. As this is affecting your area, they will not need to attend your property. Rest assured once they arrive, they’ll work as quickly as is safely possible to get your power back on.
Steps.Name: Engineers on their way

Steps.Message: Engineers are now on site, carrying out investigations into the fault. Once they have completed their assessment, they will carry out repairs.
Steps.Name: Investigating & fixing

Steps.Message: All power in the area should be back on. We’re really sorry for any disruption this caused.
Steps.Name: Power back on

PlannedIncidentReason: We're carrying out planned work in your area. For our engineers to carry it out safely they need to turn the power off. We're doing this work as it's essential to provide reliable electricity supplies to your area. We're sorry for any inconvenience caused and thank you for your patience.
MainMessage: We are currently experiencing problems with the electricity supply in your area.  We are arranging to have engineers investigate the fault.  We are sorry we can't give you a time for the restoration of supply at present. Please accept our sincere apologies for any inconvenience this may cause.
